title: CIL Geology — Advanced Mock 11 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: World's tallest railway bridge Chenab Bridge is located in:
questions.solution: Jammu & Kashmir

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Indian Bureau of Mines headquarters is in:
questions.solution: Nagpur

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Climate Change, SDGs & Environment
questions.stem: The Paris Agreement aims to limit warming to:
questions.solution: 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Founder of the Gupta dynasty was:
questions.solution: Sri Gupta

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Khelo India Youth Games 2026 were hosted in which state?
questions.solution: Assam

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Maximum strength of Lok Sabha is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is cloud computing?
questions.solution: On-demand internet-based computing resources

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Zojila Pass tunnel connecting Ladakh was completed in:

questions.stem: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme covers how many sectors?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The deepest point on Earth is:
questions.solution: Mariana Trench

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which device is used to connect a computer to a network?
questions.solution: Router

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Number of operational airports in India in 2026 was:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's GDP growth estimated by NSO for FY25-26 was:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Dead Sea is bordered by:
questions.solution: Israel and Jordan

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India ranked which position in Global Hunger Index 2026?
questions.solution: 107th

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's Deep Ocean Mission achieved a dive to what depth?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The single largest coal-producing mine in India is:
questions.solution: Gevra (SECL)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: How many Arjuna Awards were given in 2026?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Federation of Indian Mineral Industries is based in:
questions.solution: New Delhi

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The largest continent by area is:
questions.solution: Asia

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The element Germanium is named after:
questions.solution: Germany

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India was elected as a non-permanent member of UNSC for which term?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Vice-President of India is ex-officio Chairman of:
questions.solution: Rajya Sabha

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who invented the light bulb?
questions.solution: Thomas Alva Edison

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Coal & Mining in India
questions.stem: State with the largest mineable coal reserves:
questions.solution: Odisha

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A library has average 120 visitors Mon-Thu, 150 on Fri, and 200 on Sat-Sun. Weekly average is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A mixture contains milk and water in ratio 5:1. 8 L drawn off and replaced with water gives 3:1. Initial quantity:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of three different numbers is 120. Which CANNOT be their HCF?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If a = √2 - 1, then the value of a + 1/a is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If (x+y):(y+z):(z+x) = 5:7:8 and x+y+z = 20, then x =

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: How many 4-digit numbers can be formed from 0,1,2,3,4,5 without repetition?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A's income is 20% less than B's. B's is how much more than A's?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A grocer sells rice at ₹48/kg, gaining 20%. If he sells at ₹52, gain percent:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A does ¼ of work in 6 days, B does ⅓ in 8 days, C does ½ in 12 days. Together complete in:
questions.solution: 9.6 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man rows 8 km/h in still water. If stream speed is 2 km/h, time to go 15 km downstream and back:
questions.solution: 4 h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum of ₹5,000 is lent in two parts at 6% and 10% SI, total interest ₹420 in 1 year. Amount at 10%:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills a tank in 6 h. Another empties full tank in 8 h. Both open, time to fill?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A rectangular field 120 m × 84 m is to be divided into identical square plots. Minimum number of such plots:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man travels ⅓ at 20 km/h, ¼ at 30 km/h, rest at 40 km/h. Average speed:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: 12 men can do in 15 days. After 5 days, 6 more join. Remaining work takes:
questions.solution: 6.67 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If a:b = 3:4 and b:c = 5:6, then a:c is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The median of 3, 7, 9, 12, 15, 18, 21 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A conical flask radius 7 cm, height 24 cm is full of water poured into a cylindrical flask radius 14 cm. Height of water:
questions.solution: 2 cm

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 35% of a number is 175, then 65% of the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Three cubes of edges 3, 4, 5 cm are melted into one. Edge of new cube:
questions.solution: 6 cm

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Fresh grapes contain 80% water, dry grapes 20% water. Dry grapes from 120 kg fresh:
questions.solution: 30 kg

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Difference between CI and SI for 2 years at 5% on ₹10,000:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If a:b = 2:3 and b:c = 3:5, find a:b:c:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A car overtakes a bike at 90 km/h. Relative speed if bike speed is 45 km/h:
questions.solution: 45 km/h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a pie chart, sector A has 40%, B has 30%, C 20%, D 10%. If total is 360°, angle of C:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 'P > Q' means P is brother of Q, 'P < Q' means P is sister of Q, 'P = Q' means P is father of Q, then in 'A = B > C < D', how is A related to D?
questions.solution: Cannot be determined

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Abdomen : Stomach :: Skull : ?
questions.solution: Brain

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If RAT = 6, DOG = 8, then HORSE = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Number of triangles formed by 8 points on a circle:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should drone surveillance be mandatory for all opencast mines? Arg I: Yes, it improves safety monitoring. Arg II: No, it increases operational costs.
questions.solution: Both are strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Bar graph: Incomes of A=40000, B=50000, C=35000, D=45000. Ratio of C to average:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: M is the father of N. N is the mother of O. O is the brother of P. How is M related to P?
questions.solution: Grandfather

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Silence : Noise :: Stillness : ?
questions.solution: Movement

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a code, PEN = 44, BOOK = 55, ERASER = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a 5×5 square grid, how many squares?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should CIL use more contract labour in underground mines? Arg I: Yes, it reduces fixed costs. Arg II: No, it compromises safety accountability.
questions.solution: Only II is strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pie chart of 360 students: Science=40%, Arts=25%, Commerce=20%, Others=15%. Commerce students:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: P is Q's brother. R is Q's mother. S is R's father. T is S's mother. How is P related to T?
questions.solution: Great-grandson

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Celsius : Temperature :: Richter : ?
questions.solution: Earthquake intensity

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a code, COMPUTER = 593, TELEPHONE = 798. Then PHONE = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: What was the day on 26 January 1950?
questions.solution: Thursday

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Eight people sit in two rows of 4. Row 1 faces north, Row 2 faces south. P sits opposite Q. R sits 2nd left of P. S sits adjacent to Q. Who sits opposite R?
questions.solution: S

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: AY, BW, CU, DS, ?
questions.solution: EQ

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All cups are saucers. Some saucers are plates. No plate is bowl. Conclusions: I. Some cups are plates. II. Some saucers are not bowls.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a code, ABC = 6, DEF = 24, GHI = 60, then JKL = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: 3, 12, 48, 192, 768, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 30, A is 10th from left. B is 15th from right. C is exactly between A and B. Position of C from left:
questions.solution: 13th

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some red are blue. No blue is green. All green are yellow. Conclusions: I. Some red are not green. II. No blue is yellow.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He prefers coffee ____ tea in the morning.
questions.solution: to

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be at loggerheads':
questions.solution: To be in strong disagreement

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The company faced losses ____ poor management decisions.
questions.solution: due to

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To bite off more than one can chew':
options: To take on too much responsibility
questions.solution: To take on too much responsibility

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is popular ____ his colleagues.
questions.solution: among

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'Once in a blue moon':
questions.solution: Very rarely

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She was accused ____ stealing company data.
questions.solution: of

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'Bite the bullet':
questions.solution: To endure a painful situation

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government appealed ____ the public to stay calm.
questions.solution: to

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To read between the lines':
questions.solution: To understand the hidden meaning

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He has been suffering ____ fever for a week.
questions.solution: from

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be a sitting duck':
questions.solution: To be an easy target

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The patient died ____ a heart attack.
questions.solution: of

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be up in arms':
questions.solution: To protest strongly

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: ____ the circumstances, we decided to postpone.
questions.solution: Under

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To cut corners':
options: To do something cheaply or easily
questions.solution: To do something cheaply or easily

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is ____ intelligent that he solves problems instantly.
questions.solution: so

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The workers went on strike, ____ production.
questions.solution: halting

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PROCRASTINATE:
questions.solution: Delay

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ARDENT:
questions.solution: Apathetic

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'A blessing in disguise':
options: A good thing that seemed bad
questions.solution: A good thing that seemed bad

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of FERVENT:
questions.solution: Indifferent

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To let the cat out of the bag':
questions.solution: To reveal a secret unintentionally

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of TENACIOUS:
questions.solution: Yielding

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of JEOPARDIZE:
questions.solution: Endanger

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hornfels:
options: Fine-grained, massive. Contact metamorphic. Spotted variety
questions.solution: Hornfels: baked rock near intrusion. Granofels: massive granoblastic texture.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Spinel lherzolite is representative of:
questions.solution: Spinel lherzolite: olivine + orthopyroxene + clinopyroxene + spinel. Dominant upper mantle rock. Found as xenoliths.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carbonatite:
questions.solution: Carbonatite: rare, mantle-derived. Contains REE, Nb, P. Example: Oldoinyo Lengai.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geothermobarometry — GASP:
options: Grossular + Al₂SiO₅ + Quartz → Anorthite. Pressure-sensitive. Used with Grt-Bt thermometer
questions.solution: GASP: Ca transfer. K = a_An³/(a_Grs·a_Ky²·a_Qz). P from equilibrium constant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GIS — vector data model:
options: Points, lines, polygons. Attributes. Topology (shared boundaries)
questions.solution: Vector: precise, less storage (simple features). Topology: arc-node structure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Facies model — deep sea fan:
options: Inner fan (channel + levee), Mid fan (suprafan lobes), Outer fan (thin-bedded turbidites). Bouma Ta-e cycles
questions.solution: Deep fan: suprafan = depositional lobe. Channel avulsion. Growth faulting on slope.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Garnet composition series:
questions.solution: Garnet: X₃Y₂(SiO₄)₃. X = Fe²⁺, Mg²⁺, Ca²⁺. Y = Al³⁺, Fe³⁺.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Limestone is composed predominantly of the mineral:
questions.solution: Limestone is a carbonate rock chiefly of calcite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 8% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (8+25+10) = 57%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Back-arc basin formation: extension behind volcanic arc:
options: Caused by rollback of subducting slab or mantle wedge convection
questions.solution: Back-arc: Sea of Japan, Philippine Sea. Extension: graben, new ocean floor. Volcanic arc on upper plate.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Subduction zone volcanism produces:
questions.solution: Subduction: flux melting from released water. Volcanic arcs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Trilobite biostratigraphy:
options: Cambrian index fossils. Redlichia (Lower Cambrian), Paradoxides (Middle), Olenus (Upper). Extinct end-Permian
questions.solution: Trilobites: Arthropoda. Cephalon + thorax + pygidium. Holaspid (adult) segments vary.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Stromatolites represent:
questions.solution: Stromatolites: layered structures from cyanobacteria. Oldest evidence of life (~3.5 Ga). Still form today in Shark Bay.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydrothermal alteration zoning in porphyry copper:
options: Potassic → phyllic → argillic → propylitic (outward from center)
questions.solution: Potassic: K-feldspar, biotite. Phyllic: sericite, quartz, pyrite. Argillic: clay. Propylitic: chlorite, epidote.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Argon-40/Argon-39 dating is a variant of:
questions.solution: 40Ar/39Ar: irradiation converts 39K to 39Ar. Single sample, step-heating. Gives age spectrum and identifies excess Ar.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Calc-silicate rock:
options: Metamorphosed impure limestone/dolomite. Diopside, wollastonite, grossular
questions.solution: Calc-silicate: Ca-Mg silicates from silica + carbonate. Wollastonite: CaSiO3.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Sillimanite indicates:
questions.solution: Sillimanite: Al₂SiO₅ polymorph. Stable at high T. Kyanite (high P), andalusite (low P) are lower grade polymorphs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Indo-Gangetic Plain:
options: Foredeep basin. Thick alluvium (Tertiary-Quaternary). Aquifer system
questions.solution: Alluvium: Bhabhar (piedmont), Tarai (wetland), Bhangar (older), Khadar (younger).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Differential weathering produces landforms such as:
questions.solution: Differential weathering: varying resistance of rock layers creates isolated landforms. Softer rock erodes faster, harder caprock remains.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Preloading and wick drains:
options: Pre-construction settlement by surcharge + vertical drains. Accelerates consolidation
questions.solution: Wick drains: prefabricated vertical drains (PVD). Spacing 1-3m. Surcharge height ~2-6m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fold interference patterns — Ramsay types:
options: Type 0 (no interference), Type 1 (dome-basin), Type 2 (boomerang), Type 3 (hook). Superposed folding
questions.solution: Type 1: F₁ ⊥ F₂. Dome and basin. Type 2: inclined F₁. Boomerang. Type 3: co-axial F₁, F₂.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Base level of erosion:
options: Lowest level to which a river can erode. Ultimate: sea level. Local: lake, resistant rock
questions.solution: Base level: controls river profile. Changes in base level → aggradation or degradation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mantle melting — batch vs fractional:
options: Batch: complete equilibration. Fractional (Rayleigh): melt removed continuously. Trace element modeling
questions.solution: Batch: C_l/C_o = 1/(D + F(1-D)). Fractional: C_l/C_o = (1-F)^{(1/D - 1)}/D.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ore deposit types:
options: Magmatic (Cr, Pt), Hydrothermal (Cu, Zn, Pb), Sedimentary (Fe, Mn, P), Residual (Al, Ni), Placer (Au, Sn)
questions.solution: Ore: economically extractable. Grade × tonnage. Gangue: waste minerals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Subsidence — mining:
options: Longwall mining → subsidence trough. Angle of draw. Impact on structures
questions.solution: Subsidence: vertical displacement. Tilt and curvature cause damage. Mitigation: backfill, pillars.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Isotope hydrology: δ¹⁸O and δD:
options: Plot on global meteoric water line (δD = 8δ¹⁸O + 10). Deviation = evaporation
questions.solution: MWL: Craig (1961). Local MWL may differ. Evaporation shifts points below line.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diffraction: X-rays interact with crystal lattice:
options: Constructive interference when Bragg's law satisfied (nλ=2d sinθ)
questions.solution: Diffraction: crystal as 3D grating. Bragg's law: nλ = 2d sinθ. Peak positions → d-spacings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 3 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 1.3 (in the given time units). Its age is:
questions.solution: Age = number of half-lives × half-life = 3×1.3 = 3.9.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Glauconite formation environment:
options: Shallow marine, reducing conditions, slow sedimentation
questions.solution: Glauconite: green mineral. Forms in marine environments with low sedimentation rates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GIS topology: polygon overlay:
options: Intersect, union, clip, erase operations between layers
questions.solution: Overlay: combines spatial data. Intersect: AND. Union: OR. Erase: NOT. Clip: restrict to boundary.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Overexploitation indicators:
options: Declining water levels, increasing pumping cost, land subsidence, saltwater intrusion
questions.solution: Overabstraction: cone of depression expands, baseflow reduction, ecological impacts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coal seam gas content measurement:
options: Desorption canister method. Measure gas released over time, extrapolate to total gas
questions.solution: Canister: sealed coal sample, measure desorbed gas. Extrapolate using √t or diffusion model.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 45° shows a width of 20 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 20×sin45° ≈ 14.14 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pole to a plane:
options: Point on stereonet representing plane orientation (dip direction and amount)
questions.solution: Pole: perpendicular to plane. Contoured for density analysis (π-diagrams).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Disconformity:
options: Parallel beds above and below erosional surface (missing time)
questions.solution: Disconformity: missing strata due to erosion. Hard to recognize in field.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fold — anticline vs syncline:
options: Oldest rocks in core of anticline. Youngest in core of syncline
questions.solution: Anticline: upfold. Syncline: downfold. Recumbent: axial plane nearly horizontal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 30 m over a horizontal distance of 10 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(30/10) ≈ 71.6°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 10 m³ of coal, 45 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 45/10 = 4.5 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquifer — water table:
options: Boundary between saturated and unsaturated zones. Fluctuates with recharge
questions.solution: Water table: follows topography. Perched water table: above regional water table.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral that effervesces (fizzes) when dilute hydrochloric acid is applied is:
questions.solution: Calcite (CaCO₃) reacts with dilute HCl, releasing CO₂ — a standard field test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Paleochannel and terrace correlation:
options: Buried channels mapped by geophysics. Terraces coded QTI (oldest) to QTIV (youngest). Tectonic/climatic
questions.solution: Paleochannel: potential aquifer. Terrace: abandoned floodplain. Stratigraphic marker.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Unsupervised classification groups pixels by:
questions.solution: Unsupervised: ISODATA, K-means algorithms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic site classification (NEHRP):
options: A (hard rock) to F (liquefiable). Vs30 (shear wave velocity in top 30m)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: IUGS classification: QAPF diagram for plutonic rocks. Granite fields:
questions.solution: QAPF: modal mineralogy. Granite: Q 20-60%, A>P. Granodiorite: P>A. Tonalite: P>>A.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lameta Formation:
options: Infratrappean (below Deccan). Freshwater limestone, sandstone. Dinosaur fossils
questions.solution: Lameta: Maastrichtian age. Dinosaur nesting site (Jabalpur). Paleoenvironment: fluvial-lacustrine.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tidal flat facies:
options: Mudflat (clay), mixed flat (mud+sand), sandflat (sand). Bioturbation, flaser bedding
questions.solution: Tidal flat: laminae (sand-mud couplets). Flaser, wavy, lenticular bedding. Tidal channels.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granulite facies:
options: Pyroxene + feldspar + garnet. No hydrous minerals (biotite, amphibole unstable)
questions.solution: Granulite: high T, moderate P. Dehydration melting. Orthopyroxene in mafic rocks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetic anomaly over dike: symmetric vs asymmetric:
options: Vertical dike: symmetric anomaly. Dipping dike: asymmetric
questions.solution: Dike: magnetic body. Vertical: peak over center. Dipping: peak shifted toward dip direction.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Placer gold deposits form by:
options: Mechanical concentration by gravity in stream sediment
questions.solution: Placer: gold concentrated by density in alluvial environments.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rb-Sr isochron method: slope of isochron gives:
options: Age of rock (from ⁸⁷Rb/⁸⁶Sr vs ⁸⁷Sr/⁸⁶Sr)
questions.solution: Isochron: slope = e^{λt}-1. Initial ratio: y-intercept. Good for igneous rocks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: True dip vs apparent dip:
options: True dip: maximum slope. Apparent dip: any other direction (less than true)
questions.solution: True dip: perpendicular to strike. Apparent dip: trigonometric relation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 35% volatile matter and 20% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+35+20) = 41%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock sample of 100 cm³ contains 30 cm³ of pore space. Its porosity is:
questions.solution: Porosity = (void volume/total volume)×100 = 30/100 = 30%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Garnet group — pyrope:
questions.solution: Pyrope: Mg-garnet. Found in kimberlite (mantle-derived). Indicator for diamonds.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coastal — ria vs fjord:
options: Ria: drowned river valley. Fjord: drowned glacial trough
questions.solution: Ria: dendritic coastline. Fjord: steep-sided, U-shaped. Both are submergent coastlines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spatial analysis — buffer:
questions.solution: Buffer: around fault (setback), around river (flood zone). Variable buffer widths.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dilatancy angle ψ: relationship to friction angle φ in rock mechanics:
questions.solution: Dilatancy: volume increase during shear. ψ < φ. For sand ψ ≈ φ-30°. Peak: ψ + φ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lamprophyre:
options: Dark, mafic. Porphyritic with mica and amphibole phenocrysts
questions.solution: Lamprophyre: high volatile content. Mica and amphibole in groundmass of feldspar.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lithostratigraphy:
options: Rock units based on lithology (Formation, Member, Group)
questions.solution: Formation: mappable unit. Member: subdivision. Group: several formations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Petrography of carbonate sands:
options: Micrite envelope (microboring of grains) → prioritization → cementation (meniscus, pendant, blocky)
questions.solution: Micrite envelopes: formed by algal/fungal boring. Phreatic (blocky) vs vadose (meniscus) cement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Schistosity:
options: Platy mineral alignment (micas) in medium-grade metamorphic rocks
questions.solution: Schistosity: mica alignment. Schist: medium grade, visible mica flakes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic refraction — critical refraction:
options: Head wave at critical angle (sin i_c = V₁/V₂). Refractor velocity V₂ from intercept time
questions.solution: Refraction: first arrivals. Crossover distance. Layer thickness: h = (x_c/2)√((V₂-V₁)/(V₂+V₁)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 20°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 20°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 130" width="240" height="130"><polygon points="20,40 200,40 220,70 40,70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="40" y1="70" x2="220" y2="70" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="86" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">strike</text><line x1="120" y1="70" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="100" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">dip 20°</text><path d="M120 70 A 30 30 0 0 1 150 92" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
figure.caption: Strike and dip of a bed

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater age dating: tritium (³H):
options: Half-life 12.3 years. Indicates recharge after 1953 (nuclear testing)
questions.solution: ³H: radioactive tracer. Post-1953 tritium bomb pulse. Combined with ³He for younger waters.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Periglacial processes:
options: Freeze-thaw, frost heave, solifluction, ice wedges. Patterned ground
questions.solution: Periglacial: tundra. Permafrost: perennially frozen ground. Active layer: seasonal thaw.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rockfall protection: catch fence design considers:
options: Block energy (kinetic energy at impact), bounce height, runout distance
questions.solution: Energy: E = ½mv². Height of fence > bounce height. Catch ditch behind fence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Delta progradation: coarsening-upward sequence:
options: prodelta mud → delta front sand → distributary channel sand → coastal plain
questions.solution: Prograding delta: bottom-up = distal to proximal. Coarsening-upward = typical delta sequence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 60°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 60°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 130" width="240" height="130"><polygon points="20,40 200,40 220,70 40,70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="40" y1="70" x2="220" y2="70" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="86" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">strike</text><line x1="120" y1="70" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="100" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">dip 60°</text><path d="M120 70 A 30 30 0 0 1 150 92" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
figure.caption: Strike and dip of a bed

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Glacial buzzsaw hypothesis: glacial erosion limited by:
questions.solution: Buzzsaw: glacial erosion fastest near ELA. Summits eroded to ELA height.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Walther's law states that facies occurring in a conformable vertical succession also occur:
options: Laterally adjacent in the original depositional environments
questions.solution: Conformably superposed facies represent environments that were originally side by side and migrated.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biotite belongs to:
questions.solution: Biotite — black mica. One perfect basal cleavage. Common in granite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quaternary stratigraphy: Marine Isotope Stages (MIS):
options: Oxygen isotope record from foraminifera. Odd = warm, Even = cold
questions.solution: MIS: marine cores. MIS 1 = Holocene. MIS 2 = Last Glacial Maximum. MIS 5e = Last Interglacial.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Orthoclase twinning:
questions.solution: Orthoclase: K-feldspar. Carlsbad: composition plane (010).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 20 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Twinning in feldspars — Carlsbad:
options: Simple contact twin in orthoclase. Two individuals
questions.solution: Carlsbad: common in orthoclase. Simple twin with composition plane (010).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Remote sensing for geology: lineament analysis identifies:
options: Faults, fractures, joints, dikes, lithological boundaries
questions.solution: Lineaments: linear features from satellite imagery. Structural control. Mineralization often associated.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pediment vs alluvial fan: pediment is bedrock surface, fan is sediment deposit:
options: Pediment: bedrock, gentle slope. Fan: alluvial cone at mountain front
questions.solution: Pediment: etched bedrock surface. Fan: depositional. Pediment may have thin alluvial cover.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 10000 m² has 50 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 10000×50 = 500000 m³ = 0.5 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ductile shear zone — S-C fabrics:
options: S-foliation (flattening), C-foliation (shear). Angle indicates shear sense
questions.solution: S-C: S (45° to shear), C (parallel). Type I (S-C mylonites). Type II (S-C' in S-C mylonites).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal field theory explains:
options: Color and magnetic properties of transition metal minerals
questions.solution: Crystal field: d-orbital splitting. Color: d-d transitions. Magnetic: unpaired electrons.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater in hard rocks:
options: Fracture-controlled. Weathered zone (shallow) + fractured zone (deeper). Yield variable
questions.solution: Hard rock: only secondary porosity. Fracture zones, lineaments. High-yield wells along major fractures.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 10% moisture, 20% volatile matter and 8% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (10+20+8) = 62%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 45° shows a width of 10 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 10×sin45° ≈ 7.07 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Indian Gondwana stratigraphy: Lower Gondwana coal-bearing:
questions.solution: Karharbari: early Permian, good coal. Barakar: major coal seams. Talchir: glacial.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Landslide remediation: rock bolts provide:
options: Active reinforcement by compressing rock mass and increasing frictional resistance
questions.solution: Rock bolts: grouted or mechanical. Passive: resist movement. Active: pre-tensioned to compress.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Balanced cross-section requires:
options: Conservation of bed length and area during restoration
questions.solution: Balanced: no gaps or overlaps after restoration. Bed length and area preserved.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Amphibole double chain structure:
options: Si₄O₁₁⁶⁻ repeating unit. Two pyroxene chains linked
questions.solution: Amphibole: double chain inosilicate. Perfect 56°/124° cleavage. OH in structure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Perched water table:
options: Local saturated zone above region water table due to low-permeability lens
questions.solution: Perched: clay lens creates local saturation. Limited yield. Shallow wells.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granulite facies conditions:
options: T > 700°C, P 3-10 kbar. Anhydrous minerals (pyroxene, garnet, feldspar)
questions.solution: Granulite: dry conditions. H₂O absent. Pyroxene + garnet + feldspar. Lower crust.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Core drilling:
options: Obtains intact rock core. Diamond bit. Core barrel. HQ, NQ, BQ sizes
questions.solution: Core sizes: HQ (63.5mm), NQ (47.6mm), BQ (36.5mm). Recovery ratio: % core recovered.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater flow equation:
questions.solution: Steady: ∂²h/∂x² + ∂²h/∂y² + ∂²h/∂z² = 0 (Laplace). Transient: diffusion equation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gondwana basins in India:
questions.solution: Gondwana: rift basins. Lower Gondwana (Permian): coal measures. Upper Gondwana (Triassic-Jurassic): sandstones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LiDAR return types: first return = canopy top. Last return =:
questions.solution: LiDAR: multiple returns. First = top. Last = ground (if penetrates canopy). Bare earth model.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Interference colors in quartz:
questions.solution: Quartz: low birefringence → grey-white colors under crossed polars. Uniaxial positive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Miller indices (hkl):
options: Intercepts on crystal axes. (111): octahedron. (100): cube. (110): dodecahedron. Negative: bar over index
questions.solution: Miller: reciprocal of intercepts. Zero = parallel to axis. Dhkl = a/√(h²+k²+l²) (cubic).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dyke:
questions.solution: Dyke: vertical or steeply dipping. Radiating or linear swarms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermal inertia:
options: Resistance to temperature change. High for wet/dense materials. Day-night ΔT for mapping
questions.solution: Thermal inertia: √(ρ·k·C_p). Quartz: high. Water: very high. Dry sand: low. Used in geology.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A reverse (or thrust) fault is produced mainly by:
questions.solution: Compression pushes the hanging wall up over the footwall — a reverse/thrust fault.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Siwalik Group:
options: Miocene-Pleistocene. Himalayan foreland basin. Fossils: mammals (elephant, horse, ape)
questions.solution: Siwalik: sandstone, conglomerate. Himalayan uplift record. Ramapithecus, Sivapithecus.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Drainage density:
options: Total stream length / basin area. High Dd = impermeable rock, high relief, low infiltration
questions.solution: Dd < 5 km/km² (coarse) to > 500 (fine). Lithology and climate control.
